title: Sinosidus polius
taxonomy: Sinosidus polius is a genus of fossilized centipedes that date back to the Early Cretaceous period. Sinosidus polius is classified within the family of Scolopendridae, which is a suborder of centipedes. This classification places it among other scolopendrid centipedes, which are characterized by their elongated bodies and many pairs of legs. Sinosidus polius is a significant find in the field of paleontology, as it helps scientists understand the evolution and diversity of centipedes over time. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Sinosidus polius within the larger taxonomic framework.
